metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive oracle to AC #2 that confirms the correct replacement text appears, e.g. `grep -n "0\.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit naming F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6, ensuring the fix isn't just a deletion.
findings.finding: The AC #2 oracle (`grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md` shows no line describing F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70) is a negative-only check: it proves the stale text is absent but does not confirm the correct replacement text (the three-tier breakdown with 0.60/0.70/0.75) was actually written. An implementer could delete the stale sentence and pass the oracle without adding any correct threshold documentation.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC #2: 'Oracle (positive): `grep -n "0\.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit attributing the 0.60 threshold to F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add positive proving commands to AC2 that confirm the correct thresholds appear, e.g. `grep -n "0\.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep -E 'F1|E2|G5|T5e|COH|G6'` returns ≥ 1 hit, and `grep -n "0\.75" docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep 'E4'` returns ≥ 1 hit.
findings.finding: AC2's oracle only proves a negative (0.70 is absent for calibration-2 criteria) but does not prove the positive claim that the correct thresholds (0.60 for F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6; 0.75 for E4) ARE now present in the doc. The criterion says threshold statements 'match src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json' but no proving command validates the correct values exist.
findings.suggested_fix:
Extend AC2's oracle section:
```
# Positive checks
grep -nE '0\.60' docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep -E 'F1|E2|G5|T5e|COH|G6'   # must return ≥1 line
grep -nE '0\.75' docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep 'E4'                          # must return ≥1 line
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command (Oracle) for AC3, e.g. `grep -n "certified PASS" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns ≥ 1 hit, and `grep -n "remediation" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns ≥ 1 hit, so the paragraph's presence and key content are verifiable in-session.
findings.finding: AC3 has no proving command at all. It requires a paragraph to be added to docs/plan-review-gate.md but provides no oracle or grep to verify the paragraph exists and contains the required content (remediation-mode eligibility, PASS signature prerequisite, cross-reference to floor-eligibility story).
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to AC3:
```
Oracle: `grep -c "certified PASS" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns ≥ 1; `grep -c "remediation-mode eligibility" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns ≥ 1.
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an oracle to AC3 (e.g., `grep -n 'PASS signature' docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit) so the remediation-mode paragraph can be verified objectively.
findings.finding: AC3 (remediation-mode paragraph in docs/plan-review-gate.md) has no verification oracle — the criterion cannot be objectively confirmed as met.
findings.location: AC3: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md documents (one short paragraph) that remediation-mode eligibility currently requires a prior certified PASS signature'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Oracle: `grep -n "certified PASS" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit inside the new paragraph.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Augment AC2's oracle to also verify presence of the correct new thresholds (e.g., `grep -n "0\.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md` and `grep -n "0\.75" docs/plan-review-gate.md` each return at least one hit), not just absence of the old value.
findings.finding: AC2's oracle verifies only the ABSENCE of the old wrong threshold (0.70 for calibration-2 criteria) but does not verify PRESENCE of the correct replacement thresholds (0.60, 0.70, 0.75 per group) in the file.
findings.location: AC2 oracle: 'grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md shows no line describing F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2 oracle: '`grep -n "0\.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit (covering F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6); `grep -n "0\.75" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit (covering E4).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten AC1's oracle to scope the grep to the target section, e.g. `awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^## /' CLAUDE.md | grep -n "plan-review-criteria-guide"`, so a hit outside the section does not satisfy the oracle.
findings.finding: AC1's oracle (`grep -n "plan-review-criteria-guide" CLAUDE.md`) searches the entire file but the criterion requires the hit to be INSIDE the 'Ticket template the gates enforce' section. A grep hit outside that section would make the oracle pass while the AC is actually unmet (verify-command defect: substring false-positive / wrong fixture scope).
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the two grep oracles with section-scoped checks:
```
awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^## [A-Z]/' CLAUDE.md | grep -c "plan-review-criteria-guide"
awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^## [A-Z]/' CLAUDE.md | grep -c "rebar explain"
```
Each must return ≥ 1.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a note in CLAUDE.md that the criterion list is illustrative and points to criteria_routing.json as the canonical source, or add a lint/CI step that asserts the list in CLAUDE.md matches the blocking-tier entries in criteria_routing.json.
findings.finding: AC1 hardcodes the blocking-tier criterion ID list (F1, E2, G5, T5e, COH, G6, T1, T4, T8, G1G2, E4) directly in CLAUDE.md prose rather than deriving it from the authoritative source (criteria_routing.json), creating a second copy that will silently drift when criteria are added or reclassified.
findings.location: AC1 / CLAUDE.md — 'Ticket template the gates enforce' section
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the hardcoded list in CLAUDE.md with a sentence like: 'See `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` for the current canonical list; as of this writing the blocking-tier ids are: F1, E2, …' and add a TODO comment near the list instructing editors to re-sync on criteria changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a repo-wide grep step (e.g., `grep -rn '0\.70' docs/ CLAUDE.md`) to the plan or ACs to confirm no other file still describes F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 as blocking at 0.70 before closing this ticket.
findings.finding: The plan fixes the stale 0.70 threshold only in docs/plan-review-gate.md but does not audit other documentation files (e.g., README.md, CLAUDE.md itself, or other docs/) for the same stale threshold value, potentially leaving other references stranded.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or implementation note: run `grep -rn '0\.70' docs/ CLAUDE.md` and fix any additional hits that describe the calibration-2 criteria before declaring done.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `docs/experiments/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md` as an edit target (or explicitly descope it with rationale), since it currently states G1G2 and T8 are 'keep advisory' — directly contradicting the criteria_routing.json blocking thresholds the plan is documenting.
findings.finding: docs/experiments/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md is an unenumerated consumer: it explicitly states G1G2 and T8 should 'Keep advisory' (line 47–48) — contradicting criteria_routing.json which now shows them blocking at 0.70. The plan corrects docs/plan-review-gate.md but does not name this experiment doc, leaving a file that actively asserts the stale threshold policy.
findings.location: Files section (lists only CLAUDE.md and docs/plan-review-gate.md); docs/experiments/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md:47-48
findings.suggested_fix: Either add docs/experiments/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md to the Files list and add an AC to update its 'Keep advisory' line for G1G2/T8, or add a note to AC2 explicitly descoping it (e.g. 'the calibration-1 experiment doc is a historical record of the first calibration pass and is intentionally left unchanged').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale to the plan description explaining why in-place doc edits are the right approach here (e.g., targeted fix scoped to doc accuracy; auto-generation of threshold tables is deferred as a separate drift-prevention concern).
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for the chosen approach (in-place doc edits to CLAUDE.md and docs/plan-review-gate.md). The description says 'Docs-only change (no runtime behavior)' but this characterizes scope/impact, not why this approach was selected over alternatives such as auto-generating threshold text from criteria_routing.json as the source of truth.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the description: 'In-place doc edits are chosen as the minimal-blast-radius fix; auto-generating the threshold table from criteria_routing.json (to prevent future staleness) is a distinct concern tracked separately.'
